title: Phase 1.4 Completion: Postgres CI Service + Test Infrastructure

files_created.description: Postgres test harness with PGlite and real Postgres support, schema-per-test isolation
PgAdapter integration tests: query, transaction, afterCommit, Postgres-specific features

files_modified.description: Added testEffectPg() convenience function for adapter layer selection via env var
Added test:pg script for running Postgres adapter tests
Added pg-test job with PostgreSQL 16 service container

pre_existing_dirty_files_touched.delta: added test:pg script line only; pre-existing changes (import aliases, dependencies) preserved intact

schema_isolation_strategy.pglite_mode: Each call to pgTestLayer creates a fresh in-memory PGlite instance. Full isolation — no schema management needed.
schema_isolation_strategy.real_postgres_mode: Each call creates a unique schema (pg_adapter_test_N), sets search_path to isolate queries. CI creates a fresh DB per job, so schema cleanup is delegated to job lifecycle.

adapter_layer_selection.testEffectPg: When OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is set, provides PgAdapter layer with real Postgres and schema isolation. When unset, provides PGLite in-memory layer.
adapter_layer_selection.testEffect(layer): Callers provide DatabaseAdapter layer explicitly. pgTestLayer can be passed as the layer for PG-specific tests.

boundary_claim.consumer_purpose: Enable CI-backed Postgres adapter integration tests with zero-config local development via PGlite
atoms: CI pg-test job with Postgres 16 service

deferred_seams: Schema-per-test cleanup (DROP SCHEMA ... CASCADE) for real Postgres mode — CI uses fresh DB per job, no cleanup needed
Parallel test workers with shared Postgres pool — current design uses one pool per pgTestLayer call
